name: Fukuoka Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the seas (Sea of Japan and Genkai Sea) around Fukuoka and the prefecture's open sky
harmony, peace, and Fukuoka's prefectural flower (the ume / plum blossom)

description: A deep blue field charged at the centre with a white emblem in the form of a stylised plum (ume) blossom, evoking the kanji 福 of Fukuoka.
history: Fukuoka's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and adopted on 10 September 1966. The flag, with the white symbol on a deep blue field, has been in use since the same year. The ume (plum blossom) is the prefecture's official flower, associated with the Dazaifu Tenmangu shrine.

features.symbolism: the blue of the seas around Fukuoka and the prefecture's clear sky
a stylised ume (plum) blossom in five petals, the prefectural flower; the design also abstracts the kanji 福 ('fortune'), the first character of 福岡 (Fukuoka).
